title: Getting Started with Graydient Toolkit
description: Learn the basics of using the Graydient Toolkit for AI image generation
author: Graydient Toolkit Team

steps.title: Welcome to Graydient Toolkit!
steps.text:
This tutorial will guide you through the basics of using the Graydient Toolkit for AI-powered image generation.

**What you'll learn:**
• How to authenticate with the Graydient API
• How to use Telegram-style commands
• How to generate your first image
• How to customize generation parameters

steps.title: Authentication
steps.text:
First, you need to authenticate with the Graydient API.

1. Get your API key from https://app.graydient.ai/account
2. Set it as an environment variable: `export GRAYDIENT_KEY=your_key`
3. Or create a `.env` file with `GRAYDIENT_KEY=your_key`

The toolkit will automatically use this key for all API calls.

steps.title: Your First Command
steps.text:
Let's create your first image! The basic syntax is:

`/draw <your prompt here>`

**Example:**
`/draw a serene lake surrounded by mountains at sunset`

The `/draw` command uses the default image generation workflow. You can also use shortcuts like `/d`.

steps.title: Adding Parameters
steps.text:
You can customize your generation with parameters:

• `--seed <number>` - For reproducible results
• `--guidance <number>` - How closely to follow the prompt (1-30)
• `--steps <number>` - Number of generation steps
• `--width <number>` - Output width in pixels
• `--height <number>` - Output height in pixels

**Example:**
`/draw a cyberpunk cat --seed 42 --guidance 8.0 --width 1024`
steps.code_example:
# Python example
from graydient_toolkit import CommandParserEngine, InputModifier

parser = CommandParserEngine(registry)
parsed = parser.parse("/draw a cat --seed 42")
result = modifier.transform(parsed)

# Execute
output = exchange.run(result.workflow, result.params)

steps.title: Negative Prompts
steps.text:
Sometimes you want to specify what **not** to include. Use square brackets for negative prompts:

`/draw a portrait [blurry, distorted, ugly, deformed]`

You can also use multiple negative blocks:

`/draw a landscape [blurry] [low quality] [watermark]`

This helps improve image quality by telling the AI what to avoid.

media.caption: Comparison: without vs with negative prompts

steps.title: Other Commands
steps.text:
The toolkit supports many workflows:

• `/animate` or `/a` - Animate still images
• `/style` or `/s` - Apply style transfer
• `/upscale` or `/u` - Upscale image resolution
• `/img2img` or `/i2i` - Image-to-image transformation

Each command maps to a specific workflow in the Graydient API.

steps.title: You're Ready!
steps.text:
Congratulations! You've learned the basics of the Graydient Toolkit.

**Next steps:**
• Explore more workflows with `graydient-toolkit registry --list`
• Try different prompts and parameters
• Check out the tutorial editor to create your own tutorials
• Read the full documentation

Happy generating! 🎨
